beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make coffee at home in Magnolia.
beats.reasoning: Morning routine. Shaan's energy is moderate (50/100) from yesterday's late concert; he'll take a standard 30-min wake-up window and start the day fresh despite the transit strike ahead.

beats.activity: Drive from Magnolia to SoFi office on Pike Street; transit strike forces rideshare or personal car.
beats.reasoning: Metro buses and Link suspended until 4pm; Shaan must drive or rideshare. Budget 50 min for Magnolia-to-Downtown commute with possible surge traffic and mild detour around transit gaps.

beats.activity: Work: pair programming, code review, and sprint standup.
beats.reasoning: Standard morning work block. Shaan is in a good rhythm—productive pair sessions and collaborative debugging are his norm. This is a typical Thursday at the office.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place Market; grab a quick bite and walk around.
beats.reasoning: Same location as work; 0-min travel. Shaan has a pattern of grabbing lunch at Pike Place (piroshky, tacos) and enjoys the market vibe. Mild weather (68F) makes walking around pleasant.

beats.activity: Return to SoFi office; afternoon debugging, code review, and meetings.
beats.reasoning: Short walk back (same neighborhood). Afternoon work block. Transit strike doesn't lift until 4pm, so staying at the office through 5:30pm is practical before heading home.

beats.activity: Drive home to Magnolia; rideshare or personal car.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike lifted at 4pm, but rideshare surge pricing still in effect; Shaan drives. Budget 55 min for Downtown-to-Magnolia evening commute with moderate traffic.

beats.activity: Stop at QFC to pick up groceries for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Shaan is in Magnolia already; quick grocery stop fits his evening pattern. Mild weather and 68F make an outdoor errand pleasant. 15-min stop including checkout.
Short drive within Magnolia, 5–10 min; budget 20 min to account for parking and unloading groceries.

beats.activity: Cook dinner and eat at home.
beats.reasoning: Shaan's rhythm includes cooking simple dinners at home. Prep, cook, and eat takes about 70 min. Energy is moderate, so a low-key evening at home fits.

beats.activity: Wind down: text friends, tinker on side project, and relax on the couch.
beats.reasoning: Consistent with Shaan's end-of-day pattern. Mild weather and moderate energy mean a quiet evening in fits. This beat ends well before midnight, positioning him for sleep.
7.5 hours of sleep, crossing midnight and ending at 06:30 the next morning. Shaan is already home; no commute before sleep. Moderate energy from yesterday suggests decent rest needed.

diary: Woke up early enough to actually enjoy my coffee before the chaos started. The transit strike meant I had to drive myself to Pike Street—no complaints, honestly. Pair programming this morning was solid; we crushed through some gnarly async issues. Grabbed lunch at Pike Place, wandered around for a bit. The market was packed, probably everyone avoiding the strike mess. Afternoon was meetings and more code review, the usual grind. Drive back to Magnolia was smooth, which felt lucky given how packed the roads probably were. Stopped at QFC for some ingredients, threw together a decent dinner. Spent the evening tinkering on this little project I've been neglecting, texted with a couple friends. Nothing dramatic, but there's something nice about a day that just... flows.
thought_process: The transit strike actually worked in my favor—driving gave me more control over my schedule than usual. I could've stressed about surge pricing, but I chose to just take it in stride and use my car. Lunch at Pike Place was intentional; I needed a mental break, and the market always resets me somehow. The afternoon felt productive but not overwhelming, which kept my mood stable. By evening, I had enough energy left to work on my side project instead of just collapsing, which felt like a win. The day didn't demand much of me emotionally, and I leaned into that simplicity rather than pushing for something more.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
